video_title: Guy Fieri Devours Thin-Crust Pizza in Dallas 🍕 | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: Louie's
restaurants.guy_intro: Now I'm here in East Dallas, where for over 20 years, two brothers from outside of Chicago have been serving up some Midwestern family recipes at a place called Louie's. It's a bar bar. It's a real bar. I'd expect to get martinis here. What's in there? I would expect to get appliance replacement parts. Or maybe. Pick up, please. Hand cut steak. It's all about the food. Grilled pork chop with broccoli. It's fantastic. He's got pastas. He's got calzone, the best pizza in town. They put everything that they have into it. Order crab puffs. They are a couple of brothers from Waukegan, Illinois. Chris runs the kitchen. And Louie runs the front. These guys got this place rolling more than 20 years ago. And now they're a local legend for pizza Waukegan style.

dishes.description: Thin crust pizza with homemade sauce, homemade dough, topped with homemade sausage, Wisconsin cheese, onions, and green pepper, cooked at 450 degrees.
dishes.guy_response: Brother, I'm going to tell you, that's a money pie right there.

restaurants.name: Do Pizzeria Napoletana
restaurants.guy_intro: You might not think San Antonio, Texas would be home to out of bounds Italian. Well, let me just set the record straight. This place is making their own mods and perfecting the Neapolitan pie. So for those who can't make it to Italy, there's Do Pizzeria Napoletana. Owners Doug and Lori Horn are making pizza exactly the way it's done in Neapolitan, following the government guidelines in Italy to make authentic Pizzeria Napoletana.

dishes.description: Neapolitan pizza with San Marzano tomato sauce, house-made sausage, sopressata salami, house-made mozzarella, thin-sliced cheddar, and smoked prosciutto.

House-made burrata (mozzarella ribbons, reduced heavy cream) filled with sun-dried tomatoes, capers, garlic, and pesto. Served with fresh heirloom tomatoes and prosciutto di parma, drizzled with extra virgin olive oil.
dishes.guy_response: I'm telling you, I did not want to share it.

dishes.description: Pizza with melted leaf cream wine, grated fontina, pulled prosciutto, caramelized onions, rosemary-marinated olives, and fresh rosemary.
Multi-layer cake with ladyfingers soaked in house-made limoncello, ricotta filling, lemon curd, and whipped cream, topped with candied lemon zest.
dishes.guy_response: The fresh lemon flavor of it, and the candied lemon zest that he puts on top balances it out really well with a little bit of acidity. It's beautiful.

restaurants.name: Cane Rosso
restaurants.guy_intro: Dallas, Texas, in a very well-known Triple D area known as Deep Ellum. Right across the street, the new kid on the block, serving up that of pizza Neapolitana. This is Cane Rosso. Owner and chef Jay Gerrier gave up his day job, built an oven in his backyard and got certified to sling Neapolitan pies. He named it after his first dog, Cane Rosso, which means red dog in Italian.

dishes.description: Neapolitan pizza with fresh mozzarella, bacon marmalade, roasted grape tomatoes, basil, arugula, and olive oil. Cooked in a 900-degree Texas oak wood-fired oven.
dishes.guy_response: This is a whirlwind. This is, this is, this is, ah, I've lost the word. Nice job, brother.

restaurants.name: The Saucy Sicilian
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in OKC for a little pizza in the parking lot. This isn't just a regular old pizza truck, this thing is a monster with a wood-fired oven. The recipes are handed down from generations. This is the Saucy Sicilian. Gannon Mendez left the corporate world to open this food truck and asked his mom, Priscilla, to help, using his great-grandmother's recipes.

dishes.description: Wood-fired pizza with ricotta cheese, whole milk mozzarella, fresh figs, roasted garlic, roasted red pepper, topped with prosciutto and balsamic maple glaze.
dishes.guy_response: I mean, it's just really delicious. The crust is the foundation, and it's out of bounds.

dishes.description: Meatballs made with pork and beef, eggs, garlic, homemade breadcrumbs, parsley, and Parmesan. Roasted then simmered for 8 hours in Nona's marinara sauce with seared short rib.
dishes.guy_response: Delicious and tender and moist. Great sauce. A little sprinkle of the Parmesan right at the end. That fresh basil. Hits that sauce and just blooms. And that's a good amount of garlic. I'm telling you, your grandma taught you right.

restaurants.name: Aubrey J's Pizza Garage
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm on the northern side of Boulder, Colorado, to check out a pizza joint. This chef, Audrey Sherman, studied in Italy and made pizza for the greats. She combined her passion for pizza with the teachings of her mentor, Tony Gemignani, the heavyweight champ of global pizza-making. After working at his joints, she came home to open up her own with her brother Peter and put her education to work at Aubrey J's Pizza Garage.

dishes.description: Pizza with high-gluten flour dough, California tomato sauce, mozzarella, natural casing pepperoni, roasted jalapeno, house-made sausage, chopped garlic, and olive oil. Cooked in a 650-degree oven.
dishes.guy_response: The crust is outstanding. ... I can't find anything I don't like.

dishes.description: Meatballs (beef, Parmesan, breadcrumbs, oregano, garlic, eggs) roasted then added to homemade tomato sauce. Served on a fresh bread roll roasted with garlic oil, topped with Parmesan and shredded mozzarella, then baked.
dishes.guy_response: I like aggressive meatballs. We've got to have some flavor. Your mom does you right on that recipe. I like the raw garlic. After a meatball lover's meatball. More cheese than probably humanly necessary. And the roll is really the piece de resistance.
